question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: leftPadBytes(byte[] bytes, int length)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose contract slot data causes leftPadBytes() to be invoked with an attacker-controlled length value near Integer.MAX_VALUE, triggering allocation of new byte[length] and exhausting JVM heap memory, crashing the mirror node importer and halting all downstream indexing?
